verses.text: God, who spoke by the prophets, hath now spoken 'by his Son,' the 'brightness of his glory,' who purged our sins and 'sat down on the right hand of the Majesty.' Final word. The Son outshines every former messenger.

God, who spoke by the prophets, hath now spoken 'by his Son,' the 'brightness of his glory,' who purged our sins and 'sat down on the right hand of the Majesty.' Final word. The Son outshines every former messenger.

God, who spoke by the prophets, hath now spoken 'by his Son,' the 'brightness of his glory,' who purged our sins and 'sat down on the right hand of the Majesty.' Final word. The Son outshines every former messenger.
Made so much better than the angels, the Son inherits 'a more excellent name'; 'let all the angels of God worship him.' Above all. Even the highest creatures bow before the Son.
Made so much better than the angels, the Son inherits 'a more excellent name'; 'let all the angels of God worship him.' Above all. Even the highest creatures bow before the Son.
Made so much better than the angels, the Son inherits 'a more excellent name'; 'let all the angels of God worship him.' Above all. Even the highest creatures bow before the Son.
Of angels He says they are spirits, but to the Son, 'Thy throne, O God, is for ever and ever.' Worshipped God. The Father Himself calls the Son God and King.
Of angels He says they are spirits, but to the Son, 'Thy throne, O God, is for ever and ever.' Worshipped God. The Father Himself calls the Son God and King.
Of angels He says they are spirits, but to the Son, 'Thy throne, O God, is for ever and ever.' Worshipped God. The Father Himself calls the Son God and King.
'Thou, Lord, in the beginning hast laid the foundation of the earth'; the heavens shall perish, but 'thou remainest.' Unchanging. The Son made all and outlasts all.
'Thou, Lord, in the beginning hast laid the foundation of the earth'; the heavens shall perish, but 'thou remainest.' Unchanging. The Son made all and outlasts all.
'Thou, Lord, in the beginning hast laid the foundation of the earth'; the heavens shall perish, but 'thou remainest.' Unchanging. The Son made all and outlasts all.
To which of the angels said He, 'Sit on my right hand'? Angels are 'ministering spirits' sent to serve the heirs of salvation. Servants and Son. Heaven's hosts wait on those Christ saves.
